title: Epidendrum matutinum
taxonomy: Epidendrum matutinum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. The genus Epidendrum falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Epidendrum matutinum is further categorized as a member of the tribe Cymbidieae. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Epidendrum matutinum, commonly known as Morning Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum matutinum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used to make a tincture, which are the most common herbal preparations made from this plant.
